title: Klatham 'waiting in the wings'
desc: In Thailand's fluid and transactional political landscape, the emergence of the Klatham Party as a "waiting wing" opposition reflects a calibrated strategy.

title: Plastic packaging at risk as war hits crucial raw material supplies
desc: Packaging may be the next industry to bear the brunt of petrochemical supply disruptions if the Israel-US war on Iran does not abate, says Veera Kwanloetchit, a director of the Public Private Partnership for Sustainable Plastic and Waste Management.

title: Booksellers predict upbeat market
desc: The Thai book industry predicts a resilient year despite ongoing conflicts in the Middle East, according to the Publishers and Booksellers Association of Thailand (Pubat).

title: Energy security now a daunting problem
desc: Amid spikes in global crude oil and gas prices, the government has deployed several measures including price caps to ensure energy security and ease the cost of living.

title: THAI forced to increase ticket prices
desc: The war in the Middle East has driven up operating cost for Thai Airways International (THAI), with jet fuel prices rising by more than twofold, forcing the national carrier to increase average ticket prices by 10-15% to stay afloat.

title: Traders, shoppers sound price warnings
desc: Rising fuel costs linked to the Middle East conflict are driving up consumer prices, with traders warning of mounting pressure on both businesses and households.

title: Fuel panic spreads as pumps run dry
desc: Long queues at petrol stations across Thailand and "out of fuel" signs for diesel, the backbone of the transport sector, have raised public concern over the past week about the country's ability to manage energy reserves.

title: Thailand Post trumpets EV  fleet as fuel costs mount
desc: State-run logistics provider Thailand Post is emphasising replacement of petrol-powered vehicles with electric vehicles (EVs) for its last-mile delivery fleet to deal with volatile fuel prices.

title: Govt to tackle energy as 1st priority
desc: Prime Minister Anutin Charnvirakul is expected to submit his cabinet list for royal endorsement today or tomorrow, with his administration due to take office next week.

title: Ministry launches price-fixing blitz
desc: The Internal Trade Department has uncovered multiple violations after checking fuel stations and markets, warning that unjustified hikes will face legal action.

title: Ministry eyes oil excise tax trims
desc: The Finance Ministry is weighing the extent of potential oil tax cuts to keep retail fuel prices at a level the economy can absorb, while also avoiding excessive impact on government revenue.

title: Valuing electronic waste
desc: The trio of Advanced Info Service (AIS), Central Group and Japan Airlines have strengthened their collaboration on environmental, social and governance (ESG) missions to ensure an improved and sustainable future.

title: Sukhothai welcomes 'Sukh Jai Craft 2026'
desc: Sukhothai is set to welcome visitors to "Sukh Jai Craft 2026," a creative festival celebrating craftsmanship, culture, and community. Taking place from Wednesday until Sunday, between 5pm and 10pm at Prem Suk Water Park in Mueang Sukhothai, the event invites audiences to experience the province through the theme "Craft of Happiness: A City Designed for Joy."

title: Tech, services expos run jointly at Impact
desc: More than 400 domestic and international companies will present cutting-edge technologies and services at ASEE 2026 and ARHC 2026 at Impact Exhibition Centre, Muang Thong Thani, from Wednesday to Friday.

title: SCB 10X launches AI YouTube series
desc: SCB 10X has launched "AI-Volution The Series 2026", a year-long online content series for executives, entrepreneurs and anyone interested in the future of AI.

title: Two wheels over baht buses as Pattaya visitors weigh freedom against safety
desc: A foreign visitor rides a motorbike with a Thai partner through Pattaya traffic, reflecting a common transport choice among tourists seeking flexibility, even as rising fuel concerns and busy roads raise safety questions, while others still prefer walking or hopping on baht buses to get around the city. (Photo by Jetsada Homklin) PATTAYA, Thailand – For many visitors exploring Pattaya, renting or even buying a motorbike has become one of the easiest ways to get around the city. With transport costs, fuel prices, and convenience all part of the daily calculation, two wheels often win over publi

title: Rider critically hurt after losing control and crashing into tree in South Pattaya
desc: The rider, identified as a 45-year-old Thai man, lies seriously injured on the sidewalk after reportedly losing control of his motorbike and crashing into a roadside tree on Sukhumvit Road in South Pattaya. PATTAYA, Thailand – A 45-year-old motorcyclist was seriously injured after losing control of his motorbike and crashing into a tree on Sukhumvit Road in the early hours of March 22. Rescue workers from the Sawang Boriboon responded to the accident at around 4:16 a.m. near the South Pattaya traffic light intersection. At the scene, rescuers found a heavily damaged Honda Scoopy‑i motorcycle o

title: Classical music evening at Ben’s Theater Jomtien on March 26
desc: The performance will feature acclaimed musicians Eri Nakagawa on piano and Omiros Yavroumis on violin. PATTAYA, Thailand – Classical music lovers in Pattaya will have a special opportunity to enjoy an evening of chamber music when Ben’s Theater Jomtien presents a live concert at 8 p.m. on Thursday, March 26, 2026. The performance will feature acclaimed musicians Eri Nakagawa on piano and Omiros Yavroumis on violin, both associated with the Mahidol University College of Music. The evening’s programme will include works by Robert Schumann, César Franck and other composers from the cl

title: Railway parallel road project in Pattaya delayed after rain damages groundwork
desc: Workers continue construction along the railway parallel road between Khao Talo and Wat Tham intersection, where rain-soaked ground has delayed final paving and forced additional soil testing. PATTAYA, Thailand – Construction of the southern railway parallel road between Khao Talo and the Wat Tham intersection has been delayed after recent rain affected the road base, officials reported on March 22.

title: Thailand calls on public to switch off lights for global ‘Earth Hour’ on March 28
desc: Ms. Lalida Perviwatthana, Deputy Government Spokesperson, invites the public to join the global Earth Hour campaign by switching off lights for one hour on March 28.

title: Romantic retreats at The Landmark Bangkok
desc: Escape into a world of romance and refined luxury with Romantic Retreats, an exclusive stay package at The Landmark Bangkok, designed for unforgettable moments in the heart of the city.

title: Warng Warng Taiwanese Shabu: just 30 minutes drive from Pattaya
desc: Warng Warng Original Taiwanese Shabu near Phala Beach in Ban Chang, a family-owned eatery offering a refreshing alternative to the busy Pattaya and Jomtien dining scene.

title: Finnish pedestrian killed after big bike collision on Jomtien Second Road
desc: A heavily damaged Kawasaki Z800 big bike was found overturned in the road after the rider struck a pedestrian and skidded into a parked car, leaving a 55-year-old Finnish man who had been crossing the road without a pulse.

title: Heat and isolated thunderstorms expected in Pattaya this week
desc: Locals and foreign visitors ride motorbikes through Pattaya’s heat as temperatures climb above 35°C, with bottled drinking water vendors seeing brisk business along busy streets. (Photo by Jetsada Homklin)

title: Oil panic could ruin Songkran as survey shows public anxiety and weak confidence in government
desc: With the Songkran holiday approaching, a new nationwide survey suggests that concerns over a possible oil shortage and declining confidence in the government could dampen travel plans during what is normally the country’s busiest festive period.

title: Fake building permit racket uncovered on Koh Samui
desc: SURAT THANI - Authorities on Koh Samui are widening an investigation into construction permit forgery involving at least 10 projects and potentially involving both municipal staff and outsiders.

title: Travel warning: meningococcal disease reported in UK
desc: The Department of Disease Control has warned Thais travelling to the UK that an outbreak of meningococcal disease, a rare and severe bacterial infection.

title: Diesel shortages hit provinces, pumps dry
desc: Fuel shortages, particularly diesel, have intensified across several provinces, with long queues forming at petrol stations.

title: SAO details structural failures that caused tower's collapse
desc: A year after its new headquarters tower crumpled during an earthquake, the State Audit Office (SAO) has outlined four critical structural and construction failures that led to the collapse.

title: Filipino woman who just gave birth arrested after leaving newborn in black bag at Bangkok shopping mall
desc: BANGKOK — A 25-year-old Filipino woman has been arrested after the body of a newborn baby was found inside a black plastic bag in a women’s restroom at a major shopping mall on Sukhumvit Road, Bangkok. The incident came to light at 16:24 on 20 March when cleaning staff discovered the bag while performing routine trash collection in a storage room next to the women’s restroom. According to witnesses, the bag felt unusually heavy. Security personnel were immediately notified, and police, forensic teams, and medical examiners were called to investigate. Initial examination found the baby, a male,

title: Monitor lizards flourish in central Bangkok park
desc: Nicknamed the "Bangkok Dragon", the Asian water monitor has become an unexpected tourist draw thanks to its unusually calm, non-aggressive nature.  These lizards are widespread beyond Lumpini Park, thriving along canals and wetlands across the capital and even appearing near Government House — where, some quip, honest politicians diligently work for the people.

title: 首次曝光！泰国被罢黜王子公开长女
desc: 泰国头条新闻社讯 3月21日，泰国瓦查勒颂王子在其个人社交媒体账号上发布图文，首次公开其长女照片，并为女儿送上生日祝福。这也是他首次通过社交媒体向外界披露女儿的形象。

title: Government says Thailand records three meningococcal deaths
desc: Government says Thailand records three meningococcal deaths Legacy The Thai government said today, March 22, that it has instructed the Department of Disease Control (DDC) to closely monitor an outbreak of meningococcal disease in the United Kingdom, with three deaths reported in Thailand .

title: Poll: Doubts over oil reserves, Songkran travel at risk
desc: A sizeable share of Thais lack confidence that the country’s oil reserves will last the promised 98 days or that the government can secure additional supplies, and some will cancel Songkran travel plans if the crunch persists, according to a survey by the National Institute of Development Administration (Nida).

title: Police called after naked man walks into Khon Kaen petrol station
desc: Police in Khon Kaen took a man into custody and sent him to the hospital today, March 22, after staff at a petrol station reported a naked man inside the premises, with rescuers saying he may have been experiencing a mental health episode .

title: Man alleges gunpoint abduction and assault linked to ex’s family
desc: Man alleges gunpoint abduction and assault linked to ex’s family Legacy A man in Nonthaburi filed a police complaint on March 20 after he was allegedly taken from outside his home by three men linked to his ex-girlfriend’s family in a pickup truck and later assaulted in the early hours of March 19 The man, identified as 31 year old Nawi said he reported the incident after seeking medical treatment for serious injuries.

title: Swedish man attacked by transgender woman in Pattaya
desc: Swedish man attacked by transgender woman in Pattaya Legacy A 59 year old Swedish man was injured after being assaulted by a transgender woman in Jomtien, Pattaya , Chon Buri , in the early hours of Saturday, March 22.

title: Thailand to cut visa-free stay to 30 days, Foreign Ministry says
desc: Thailand to cut visa-free stay to 30 days, Foreign Ministry says Legacy Thailand’s Foreign Ministry is preparing to ask the government to cut the visa-free stay for eligible foreign tourists from 60 days to 30 days.

title: 泰国商业部召集9大零售巨头，商讨物价管控
desc: 泰国头条新闻社讯 3月21日，泰国国内贸易厅厅长威他功在与9家大型消费品企业会谈后表示，为评估中东冲突对原油相关原料和货运的影响，国内贸易厅与联合利华、协成昌、宝洁等就塑料包装和塑料瓶等产品运输延误可能导致的供应紧张进行了商讨。

title: Thailand plans to cut visa-free stay for foreigners from 60 to 30 days
desc: BANGKOK — Thailand’s Ministry of Foreign Affairs is preparing to propose reducing the visa-free stay period for foreign tourists from 60 days to 30 days, citing concerns over misuse of the scheme.

title: 警惕脑膜炎！泰国今年已有3例死亡
desc: 泰国头条新闻社讯 3月22日，泰国总理府办公室副发言人艾琳透露，政府已指示公共卫生部疾病控制厅密切关注英国侵入性脑膜炎双球菌感染疫情的发展。泰国国内从今年1月1日至3月17日，共发现5例病例，其中3人死亡。

title: Bangkok arrests 16 foreign women over alleged prostitution
desc: Bangkok arrests 16 foreign women over alleged prostitution Legacy A late-night prostitution crackdown in Bangkok led to 16 arrests on March 20 after complaints on social media alleged a group of foreign women were approaching visitors to offer sexual services on Sukhumvit Soi 4.

title: Which country is happiest in Southeast Asia in 2026?
desc: The annual rankings, compiled by Gallup, the Oxford Wellbeing Research Centre and the UN Sustainable Development Solutions Network... In Southeast Asia, Singapore...

title: 泰国严打旅游房地产“代持”，4家旅行社被吊销执照
desc: 泰国头条新闻社讯 3月21日，泰国商业部商业发展厅厅长普蓬透露，在春武里府芭提雅市展开联合行动，对旅游及相关服务业、房地产业的疑似“人头公司”进行清查。4家旅行社被吊销执照。

title: Thailand oil crisis poll shows unease and doubts over reserves
desc: Thailand oil crisis poll shows unease and doubts over reserves Legacy A new NIDA Poll released today, March 22, found that many adults in Thailand are worried about the country’s oil crisis , with more than half reporting daily difficulties, and a large share expressing little confidence in the government’s ability to maintain a stable oil supply . The survey, titled “Oil shortage: Talk to your partner, Switch off the lights”, was conducted by the NIDA Poll centre at the National Institute of Development Administration between March 17 and 18. It reportedly questioned 1,310 people aged 18 and

title: Samut Sakhon fishing crisis: fuel price hike forces boats to dock
desc: Samut Sakhon fishing crisis: fuel price hike forces boats to dock Legacy Samut Sakhon fishermen face dockings as ‘green oil’ price rises 6.5 baht per litre The Samut Sakhon fishing industry is under mounting financial pressure as the price of ‘green oil’ rises by 6.5 baht per litre on March 22, forcing many fishermen to dock their boats and prompting growing calls for government intervention. Mongkhon Mongkhontrilak, chairman of the Samut Sakhon Fisheries Association, said the price will rise from 34.3 baht to 40.8 baht per litre for cash purchases, with credit purchase

title: 泰国计划开发邮轮码头，助力建设海上旅游枢纽
desc: 泰国头条新闻社讯 3月21日，泰国交通部与皇家加勒比集团（RCG）举行会谈，共同讨论在泰国湾和安达曼海建设邮轮码头的规划，旨在解决现有设施拥挤的问题，同时提升接待大型邮轮的能力，带动国家经济，推动泰国成为区域海上旅游中心。本次会谈由交通部次长潘亚主持，皇家加勒比集团亚洲区公共关系部副总监Wendy Yamazaki和泰国港务局（PAT）的代表参会。 潘亚表示，泰国政府重视全球邮轮旅游快速增长的趋势，正全力开发大型邮轮码头，并通过公私合营模式，吸引民营企业参与建设与运营。目前已锁定苏梅岛、普吉岛和泰国湾上部等区域，同时正在评估芭提雅和梭桃邑之间的最佳位置，以扩大全球旅游网络至泰国东西两岸海域。 作为全球领先的邮轮公司，RCG拥有69只船队，航线遍布世界各地。今年RCG计划安排超45个邮轮航次停靠泰国港口，预计将吸引约12万名游客入境。然而，海上旅游的扩张也带来了相应挑战，双方就解决缺乏符合国际标准的大型邮轮码头这一问题坦诚交换了意见。为了让投资方提前做好准备，交通部正在整理林查班港的发展规划，以及其他高潜力区域的邮轮码头项目阶段计划表，准备提交给RCG和其他潜在投资者，供他们参考及进行后续的商业布局。 （编译：KA；来源：posttoday） The post 泰国计划开发邮轮码头，助力建设海上旅游枢纽 appeared first on 泰国头条新闻 .

title: 1.3 million meth pills worth 39 million baht found by Mekong
desc: 1.3 million meth pills worth 39 million baht found by Mekong Legacy A village security team patrolling the banks of the Mekong River in Nong Khai province discovered three large packages containing around 1.3 million methamphetamine pills worth over 39 million baht on March 20. Police believe the drugs were part of a trafficking network’s attempt to move narcotics deeper into the country. The find was announced at a press conference at the Sangkhom district office on March 21. Nong Khai governor Saransak Srikruanet, provincial police commander Atthachon Chuangkan, Sangkhom district chief

title: Department of Disease Control targets online nicotine pouch sellers
desc: Department of Disease Control targets online nicotine pouch sellers Legacy Thailand’s Department of Disease Control (DDC) has lodged a complaint over the alleged online sale of nicotine pouches , after monitoring found the products were being advertised and sold digitally, with further checks pointing to a physical shop in Pathum Thani . Speaking yesterday, March 21, deputy director-general of the DDC Anek Moong-om-klang said that on March 17, he assigned investigators at Mueang Nonthaburi Police Station to pursue legal action against those accused of selling nicotine pouches online. The DDC s

title: 泰国寺庙面临“油缺”，遗体或无法及时火化
desc: 泰国头条新闻社讯 3月21日，泰国北榄府多个地区的寺庙正面临柴油严重短缺的问题，而柴油是火化仪式不可或缺的燃料，进而导致宗教仪式无法正常进行。部分寺庙已开始考虑改用传统柴火焚烧，或先将遗体冷藏保存暂延火化...（略）

title: 6人被捕，泰警捣毁一中资电诈窝点
desc: 泰国头条新闻社讯 3月20日，泰国第一警区、暖武里府地方警察局、Pakkred警察局联合宣布，成功捣毁一中资电信诈骗窝点，缴获总值超1400万泰铢的涉案物资，并逮捕6名嫌疑人，包括31岁的中国籍男性主犯WANG QINSEN...（略）

title: “阿努廷2.0”或下周正式就职，4月7-9日发布施政方针
desc: 泰国头条新闻社讯 3月22日，泰国总理阿努廷预计将于3月24日之前将“阿努廷2.0内阁”名单呈请国王御准...（略）

title: 泰国打击跨国废物走私获联合国点赞
desc: 泰国头条新闻社讯 3月22日，联合国毒品和犯罪问题办公室（UNODC）于近日发文称，泰国3月10日在林查班港成功查获了一批非法进口电子废物...（略）
